source.title: Complementary learning systems within the hippocampus: a neural network modelling approach to reconciling episodic memory with statistical learning.
source.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B

confidence_rationale: The architectural-resolution paper for the labeled-line vs distributed-coding tension. Models the hippocampus as having TWO pathways with different computational roles: monosynaptic (entorhinal -> CA1 direct) supports statistical learning / similarity-preserving regularities; trisynaptic (entorhinal -> DG -> CA3 -> CA1) supports per-episode pattern separation / labeled-line-like specificity. This is direct biological licensing for OPTION C (hybrid) in the SD-049 verdict: a substrate with both a similarity-preserving distributed embedding pathway AND a labeled-line-like per-instance pathway. Source quality high (peer-reviewed Phil Trans, Schapiro/Norman/Botvinick lab triumvirate). Mapping fidelity strong: the architectural commitment is exactly what option C licenses. Transfer risk moderate -- model is hippocampus-internal architecture, REE encoder is a single MLP head, so the bi-pathway architecture would need to be re-instantiated at the encoder level rather than transplanted.
failure_signatures: Option A choice produces strong per-cell pattern-separation (good for labeled-line / one-hot identity) but no similarity-preserving statistical-learning capacity. Predicted V3-EXQ-514 signature: high identity-recovery accuracy + low cross-cell-instance generalisation = trisynaptic-only failure mode in this paper's framework.

Option B choice produces strong similarity-preserving distributed coding but loses per-instance specificity. Predicted signature: low identity-recovery + good similarity-structure inferences = monosynaptic-only failure mode. The wanting/liking dissociation MECH-229 cares about may pass on similarity grounds but fail on identity-recovery.

Option C hybrid licensed by this paper would predict that BOTH signatures pass: high identity-recovery + similarity structure preserved. The architecture is biologically grounded in two known anatomical pathways. If V3-EXQ-514 ARM_2 fails on either dimension, the architecture may need explicit two-head implementation rather than a single z_resource encoder.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A neural network model instantiating known properties of hippocampal projections shows that the trade-off between learning episodes (per-instance specificity) and learning regularities (similarity-preserving statistical structure) can be handled by separate anatomical pathways within the hippocampus. The monosynaptic pathway (entorhinal cortex -> CA1 direct) supports statistical learning by allowing distributed similarity-preserving representations to develop. The trisynaptic pathway (entorhinal -> dentate gyrus -> CA3 -> CA1) supports per-episode pattern separation through DG sparsification and CA3 recurrence. Apparent representations of regularities emerge from the trisynaptic side via associative reactivation through recurrence, but the specialized statistical-learning pathway is monosynaptic.
mapping.ree_translation: The biological architecture for reconciling labeled-line per-instance coding (option A) with distributed similarity-preserving coding (option B) is to have BOTH pathways. SD-049 option C hybrid is the direct architectural translation: a one-hot identity-classifier head supervised on resource-type tags (analog of trisynaptic pattern separation), backed by a distributed learned embedding that develops similarity structure across types (analog of monosynaptic statistical-learning pathway). The two pathways share input but produce different read-outs serving different computational roles. REE z_resource encoder under option C would have an identity-classifier head AND a similarity-rich embedding component, with downstream consumers reading whichever fits their needs.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Schapiro model is anatomically specific to hippocampus (DG, CA3, CA1, entorhinal). REE's z_resource encoder is a single MLP head, not a recurrent multi-pathway network. The architectural primitive (have two complementary pathways) transfers, but the specific anatomical instantiation does not. A clean Phase 2 implementation of option C would need to commit to what 'two pathways' means at the encoder layer -- e.g. shared backbone with separate identity-classifier and embedding heads, vs entirely independent encoders. Phase 2 design needs to make this explicit.
mapping.source_context: Computational neural-network model of hippocampal subfields trained on sequences exhibiting community structure; comparison of monosynaptic-only, trisynaptic-only, and full-circuit ablations; mapping of network behaviour to fMRI findings from the Schapiro 2016 paper (cross-tag entry).

confidence_components.notes: The Schapiro/Norman/Botvinick collaboration is canonical for CLS modeling, and this paper provides the architectural resolution of the labeled-line vs distributed-coding tension that runs through the rest of the slate. Mapping fidelity strong because the option-C hybrid REE wants to license is precisely the bi-pathway architecture this paper instantiates. Transfer risk modest because the architectural primitive is general.
